candidates.stmt: I WILL NOT TRY AND PERSUADE YOU TO VOTE FOR ME WITH ELECTORAL CAMPAIGN PROMISES AND STATEMENTS. THIS IS ME! I am a wife, and mother of two serving police officers and a grandmother of two. I have lived in Rochford in the Roche North & Rural ward for over 25 years. When I first moved to Rochford it was a lovely semi-rural town full of history and wonderful neighbours. Sadly, that is not the Rochford that I see today. Over the last 4 years I have worked with the Rochford Supporters Group trying to fight the Bloor development opposite Holt Farm Schools. During these 4 years I have come to realise that our Council is not supportive of its residents and have encountered avoidance of matters of concern, lack of engagement from officers who should be engaged and negligence. There has been little engagement when dealing with serious issues from some not all of the local Councillors. This has made me realise change needs to happen and the only way to help make those changes is to stand as an Independent Candidate in the hope the residents will trust me enough to place their vote in my favour. I will not make promises to Boost this or improve that or Protect Our or Provide for or Reduce or Increase anything. Often these are empty election claims and the end product rarely manifests itself. What I will promise is to tell you the truth at all times. I will do my best to support any efforts that would benefit the community and at the same time ensure that your money is spent in an efficient and productive way. I am not afraid to put my head above the parapet and make my voice heard. If you come to me with a problem, I won’t just report it as is the way of others. I will pursue the issue to its conclusion. You have my word. As an individual I am constantly ignored but as a Councillor I will be able to represent you all in an official capacity which according to the constitution gives me a voice. Standing alone as an Independent I do not have to toe a party line nor do I have to f

Since first being elected as a Councillor in 2019, I have developed a passion for our District. I have worked tirelessly to represent our residents, businesses and stakeholders at a local and National level.

Most notably, I became leader of the Council in May, the first women and youngest ever leader of RDC, and instantly took the fight to Government to challenge their ridiculous housing targets. I am prepared to defend our Green Belt with everything I have. I have been consistent with my views and my approach.

But being a Cllr isn’t just about the big things, it’s arguably just as important dealing with the case work, and helping residents with the things that matter most, be it a missed bin collection, or a major housing issue, I have proven consistently I will help, even when it’s not in my remit to do so.

I have diarised my journey on Facebook through the years, so that you can see I woke all year round, year in, year out - not just when there is an election.

I take the party out of politics and just work for the residents I represent, and have done so this year in collaboration with two Independant groups, for the good our district.

My name is John-Barry Waldron and I'm excited to be running as a candidate for the green party in the upcoming local elections. I am passionate about preserving the local environment and green spaces. Working with the local community to advocate for them on local issues. 

As a mental health nurse, I have witnessed the impact that cuts to local services have had on the mental health of individuals and the community. That’s why I am committed to making mental health a priority in this local election, including ensuring that everyone has better access to local services and resources. 

I believe that the environment, local services and mental health are interconnected and that by prioritising them, we can create a stronger, more resilient community that is able to face the challenges of the future. 

As a candidate for the green party, I am committed to working hard on behalf of the community and strongly supporting policies that will support the well-being of all of us. I am excited to work with you and to make a positive difference in the community.

Thank you for considering my candidacy and I look forward to earning your support in this election.

“I have lived in Hockley all his life, attended Hockley Primary then Greensward Academy before going on to have a successful career as a professional chef, working in some of the area’s most popular restaurants, pubs and hotels before moving into the care sector, cooking for people with severe disabilities & in supported living schemes”

"During the pandemic, as a key worker I travelled all over the county trying to source food for the Nursing home so that the resident always had meals during this difficult time. In 2023 I joined Hockley Parish Council and championed the Stand-up to Racism campaign in an effort to help combat a string of racist graffiti around Hockley and Hawkwell." 

"When Hockley Station was being threatened with closure, I stood on the picket line with RMT members, spoke with the public and handed out leaflets, which help lead to the station being saved with over 800 people objecting to the closure."

"I have also had spent a number of years helping raise money for various charities through online platforms, such as Twitch and YouTube. As such I have raised thousands of pounds for charities such as Macmillan cancer support, Diabetes UK, Cats Protection League and Guide Dogs UK."

"Early this year I stood as the green candidate for Hockley for District Council elections, beating out Labour and only 170 votes behind the Conservatives. This was the first time a Green has ever stood in Hockley as showed that I can get results."

Shona works part-time in the NHS, is a passionate advocate for older people's rights, a youth group leader, and a volunteer with local families and children. She will be a tireless champion for Roche South and the passionate voice the area has lacked for so long.

I am standing in the Rochford District Council elections on 5th May, 2022 for Wheatley Ward in Rayleigh. Born in Rayleigh and always having lived here, I have seen many changes over the years, some to the detriment of Rayleigh people. Whatever your political preference is at the national level, local politics is a completely different entity but equally as important. Decisions taken at the local government level, greatly affect our quality of life and our community's well-being both now and for future generations. Even if you don't vote for me, I passionately believe it is our duty to vote out of respect to the many millions who lost their lives in countless conflicts to maintain our democracy and way of life. Like my parents before me, I have always voted Liberal and latterly Lib Dem as I believe it is important to have a balanced approach to politics.

I have lived in Rayleigh for all of my life, and I want to contribute to improving our community. This election is of major importance for our local area. I am focused on delivering on local issues, not national issues which have nothing to do with my role as a potential Councillor. I am standing to protect Wheatley’s green belt and achieve an effective local plan. I live right in between two of the potential green belt sites that could built on in Wheatley, and so this would have a big impact on me and residents alike.

Due to the way the composition of the Council currently is, along with only 1/3rd of seats up for election - as is the norm, this means that it is only the Conservatives that can effectively deliver on the things that matter to you. This is especially so with how relations between local parties currently are.

To deliver a local plan to protect our green belt, we need a stable, united administration. This is the final District Council election before local government reorganisation takes place in 2 years time. This is why we need an effective local plan - which is currently in the works - and it needs to be finalised very soon. For the reasons provided, I hope you could lend me your vote on the 7th May, even if you may not normally vote Conservative. Thank you.
